prompt: Please do the task stated in `/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2_generation_prompts.md`, where the TASK_ID is `f9d67f8b`.
codex_response:
Implemented `task_f9d67f8b`.

The rule I used is: treat the grid as a 30x30 crop of a 32x32 pattern mirrored across both center lines, and replace each `9` from its bilateral symmetry orbit when that orbit still exposes a non-`9` value. The Opus hint was a useful start; the Sonnet hint was discarded, and [notes.md](/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2/tasks/task_f9d67f8b/notes.md) records the correction plus the official held-out ambiguity. No new shared primitive was needed.

Validation is complete: `python -m arc2.shared.verify --task f9d67f8b` passes `5/5`, and `python -m arc2.shared.build --task f9d67f8b --n-examples 1000` produced the full artifact set. I checked the generated preview sheet against the originals and revised the generator so it makes larger structured symmetric motifs with a few big `9` hole clusters instead of pure noise.
